No isolated behavioral unit under test — verified by the walkthrough smoke gate + a concrete byte-equivalence proof (default-unset → identical argv).

Byte-equivalence proven concretely: with `KAOLA_FINALIZE_BASE` unset, the constructed argv is exactly `[0]=$PLAN [1]=--barrier-check [2]=--json` (0 trailing args via `"${BARRIER_BASE_ARG[@]}"` empty-array expansion) — textually identical to the pre-edit `node "$VALIDATOR" "$PLAN" --barrier-check --json`; with it set, argv appends `[3]=--base [4]=<ref>`. Cross-edition parity: all 4 surfaces carry `"${BARRIER_BASE_ARG[@]}"` (canonical/GitLab/Gitea use `$VALIDATOR`+2-space indent; Codex SKILL uses `$validator_script`+flush-left — pre-existing variable/indent difference, identical forwarding logic).
